cards.name: Dulcinea Panaderia Oaxaquena
cards.address: 795 S Second St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Dulcinea Panaderia Oaxaquena — grab a coffee and a fresh pan dulce or a savory pastry to kick things off right.
cards.why: An actual Oaxacan bakery open this early is a rare find and worth the stop.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail along the creek path — it's shaded, flat, and genuinely peaceful on a cool spring morning like this one.
cards.why: 59 degrees and sunny is basically perfect walking weather.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum — real mummies, ancient artifacts, and hieroglyphics in the middle of San Jose. Walk the garden too.
cards.why: It's genuinely one of the most surprising places in the South Bay and a great way to spend a Friday afternoon.

cards.name: SJSU First-Year Writing Program's Digital Literacy Showcase at MLK Library
cards.blurb: Check out the SJSU First-Year Writing Program's Digital Literacy Showcase at MLK Library — student work on digital culture and media, free and open to the public.
cards.why: It's a free, low-key cultural stop that fits perfectly between lunch and dinner.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll Santana Row as the evening starts to warm up — browse the boutiques, people-watch on the main boulevard, and scope out a dinner spot.
cards.why: Friday evening energy here is hard to beat.

cards.name: Cap't Loui
cards.address: 19700 Vallco Pkwy #150, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Cap't Loui in Cupertino for dinner — try the local favorite dishes and settle in for a proper Friday night meal.
cards.why: A well-loved spot that makes for a satisfying end to the day.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with an easy stroll along the creek trail. The path is shaded, flat, and totally doable for little legs — let the kids run ahead while you wake up properly.
cards.why: 59 degrees and sunny is basically perfect trail weather for a Friday morning.

cards.name: Antoine’s Cookie Shop Los Gatos
cards.address: 35 W Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Antoine's for fresh-baked cookies that the kids will absolutely lose it over. Grab a box to share and let everyone pick their own.
cards.why: It's a treat they'll be talking about the whole rest of the day.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Real mummies, ancient Egyptian artifacts, and hieroglyphics in the middle of San Jose. Kids go wide-eyed in the tomb replica, and the gardens outside are worth a slow walk after.
cards.why: It's low-key one of the coolest things you can do with kids in the South Bay for under $12.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Head out to Vasona for the afternoon. The kids can ride the little train, explore the lake shoreline, and burn through whatever energy they have left. Bring a blanket and just hang.
cards.why: The high hits 73 today, which makes this the ideal afternoon outside with the family.

cards.name: Friday Fun
cards.address: 1157 Minnesota Ave San Jose
cards.blurb: Friday Fun at the San Jose Public Library branch on Minnesota Ave. Free, kid-focused, and a great low-key wind-down activity after an active afternoon.
cards.why: Free programming on a Friday evening is a gift — easy yes for the kids.

cards.name: Yopop
cards.address: 5353 Almaden Expy, San Jose, CA 95118, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Yopop for frozen yogurt. Let the kids go wild with toppings and call it dessert-as-dinner energy. You've earned a low-key finish.
cards.why: It's affordable, the kids will love picking their own toppings, and it wraps the day on a high note.

cards.name: Fleur De Cocoa
cards.address: 39 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Fleur De Cocoa on North Santa Cruz Ave. Grab a coffee and something pastry-shaped to ease into the day.
cards.why: It's a legitimately good morning stop and the weather is perfect for sitting outside with something sweet.

cards.name: Coffee with the Mayor
cards.blurb: Head over to Saratoga for Coffee with the Mayor. Show up, ask something, see what's going on in town.
cards.why: Free, low-key, and kind of fun to peek into local politics over a cup of coffee.

cards.name: The Everly Set
cards.address: 15400 Montalvo Rd, Saratoga
cards.blurb: Catch The Everly Set at Montalvo Arts Center. Grab your spot and settle in for a midday show at one of the nicest outdoor venues in the area.
cards.why: 72 degrees and a live show at Montalvo is basically a perfect Friday afternoon.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch on the Los Gatos Creek Trail. It's shaded, flat, and follows the creek the whole way. Good pace for a Friday afternoon stretch.
cards.why: A mellow walk after a show feels exactly right on a day like this.

cards.name: Loma Brewing Company
cards.address: 130 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Post up at Loma Brewing Company on North Santa Cruz Ave for happy hour drinks and something solid to eat. The patio is the move when the weather cooperates.
cards.why: Good beer, good food, and you'll want to decompress after a full afternoon.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 37 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night at Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge on North Santa Cruz Ave. Paint something, zone out, and let the Friday energy wind all the way down.
cards.why: It's open late and doing something with your hands after a busy day is genuinely relaxing.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Vasona Lake with a walk along the water and let the kids burn some energy on the trails. If the little train is running, grab a ride around the park.
cards.why: Cool morning temps and a gorgeous lake make this the perfect way to kick off a Friday with kids.

cards.name: Big E Cafe
cards.address: 1683 Branham Ln, San Jose, CA 95118, USA
cards.blurb: Swing into Big E Cafe for a casual, affordable lunch. Great spot for a laid-back midday meal with the family.
cards.why: Wallet-friendly and low-key, which is exactly what you want after a morning outside.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Hop on the Los Gatos Creek Trail and follow the path along the creek. It's flat, shaded, and great for little legs on bikes or foot.
cards.why: Easy and scenic, and the kids will love having a whole trail to explore.

cards.name: Art Beat Studio
cards.address: 68 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Art Beat Studio is a hands-on creative space where kids can make something and take it home. Let them pick a project and go for it.
cards.why: Kids love making stuff, and this gives them a creative outlet after all that outdoor time.

cards.name: Art Studio by Toys Natural
cards.address: 145 Dillon Ave Suite H, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Art Studio by Toys Natural opens at 2 and is a fantastic afternoon stop for creative play with an art and toy focus. Let the kids dig in.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun and imaginative space that holds kids' attention for a solid stretch of the afternoon.

cards.name: Jack's Restaurant & Bar
cards.address: 1502 Saratoga Ave, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Jack's Restaurant and Bar is a solid dinner spot with a casual vibe and a menu that works for the whole family. Settle in and let someone else do the cooking.
cards.why: After a full day, a sit-down dinner where the kids are welcome is exactly what the family needs.

cards.name: Steve Jobs in Exile
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Catch the Steve Jobs in Exile exhibition while you're already at the museum. It covers the years between his departure from Apple and his return, which is honestly the most interesting chapter.
cards.why: You're already there, so knock out both before lunch.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Walk off the museum time with a lap around Shoreline Lake. The weather's perfect for it, and the Bay views are surprisingly good on a clear spring morning.
cards.why: 72 degrees and no rain means you actually want to be outside today.

cards.name: The City Fish
cards.address: 21678 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to The City Fish for lunch. It's a solid seafood spot with fresh fish dishes at a price that won't hurt.
cards.why: Good seafood at this price point is hard to find in the South Bay.

cards.name: FireWorks Ceramics
cards.address: 483 Hope St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into FireWorks Ceramics in Mountain View for an afternoon session. Whether you're throwing on the wheel or trying hand-building, it's a genuinely fun way to spend a Friday afternoon.
cards.why: It's a refreshingly different thing to do on a Friday and you'll walk out with something to show for it.

cards.name: Books to Blooms: A Garden Lover’s Party
cards.address: 265 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022
cards.blurb: Swing over to Los Altos for Books to Blooms, a garden lover's party at 265 State St. It's free and a low-key, pretty setting to ease into the evening.
cards.why: It's free, it's Friday, and it's the kind of thing that's fun to stumble into.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Let the kids run loose along the waterfront. Walk the trail around the lake, watch the birds, or rent a bike if you're feeling it. The bay views are genuinely great on a clear morning.
cards.why: Fresh air and open space first thing sets the whole day up right.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Dig into the exhibits at the Computer History Museum. The robot section alone will keep kids glued, and the timeline of computing history is surprisingly wild to walk through together.
cards.why: It's legitimately fascinating for all ages, not just the tech nerds in the group.

cards.name: The Future of Humanoid Robots
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Check out the Humanoid Robots exhibition while you're already at the museum. Gets into where robotics is actually headed, and kids tend to lose their minds over it.
cards.why: You're already inside, and this one is worth the extra time.

cards.name: State of Mind Public House and Pizzeria
cards.address: 101 Plaza N, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Head over for pizza at State of Mind. Order a pie to share at the table and let everyone decompress after the museum.
cards.why: Pizza is always the right call with kids, and this place does it well.

cards.name: Sylvan Park
cards.address: 600 Sylvan Ave, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the afternoon at Sylvan Park. There's room to kick a ball around, hit the playground, or just sit while the kids burn off that pizza energy.
cards.why: The weather today is perfect for a late-afternoon park run.

cards.name: Books Inc.
cards.address: 317 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Books Inc. on Castro Street. Let the kids pick something out and browse the shelves. Good way to wrap a Friday before heading home.
cards.why: A bookstore visit is a low-key, genuinely fun way to end the day with kids.

cards.name: Vegan Bistro Donuts & Cafe
cards.address: 449 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with coffee and a donut at this beloved cafe on East Santa Clara — the kind of spot that makes you slow down and actually enjoy breakfast.
cards.why: Perfect way to ease into a Friday with something genuinely good.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours walking through real Egyptian artifacts, actual mummies, and detailed hieroglyphics in one of the most surprising museums you'll find anywhere in California.
cards.why: You'll be talking about this one for weeks.

cards.name: AI as a tool of dispossession
cards.blurb: Head over to SJSU for this free community talk on AI and dispossession. It's the kind of conversation that makes you think differently about tech and who it actually serves.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a great use of a Friday afternoon.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk through the Municipal Rose Garden while the afternoon warms up. Spring is the best time to catch the blooms, and it's completely free.
cards.why: It's gorgeous out and this is one of the most beautiful free spots in the South Bay.

cards.name: SJSU Dance - Flourish at Spartan Complex Central
cards.blurb: Catch the SJSU Dance Flourish showcase at Spartan Complex Central for a free afternoon performance featuring student dancers.
cards.why: Free live performance and a fun way to spend a Friday afternoon.

cards.name: Coterie Winery
cards.address: 885 W Julian St, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening at Coterie Winery with a glass or two in a relaxed, intimate setting — the kind of place you linger longer than planned.
cards.why: A Friday night wine stop that actually feels special.

cards.name: Philz Coffee
cards.address: 118 Paseo de San Antonio, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a Philz blend the kids can smell from the door — grab a mint mojito coffee for the adults and a hot chocolate or pastry for the little ones and ease into the day.
cards.why: A chill, cozy way to kick off a Friday without rushing.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Real mummies, ancient coffins, and actual hieroglyphics inside a planetarium-style building in San Jose — kids absolutely flip for this place. Walk through the replica Egyptian tomb and let everyone take their time in the artifact galleries.
cards.why: It's genuinely one of the coolest kid surprises in the whole South Bay.

cards.name: San Jose Municipal Rose Garden
cards.address: Municipal Rose Garden
cards.blurb: Walk off lunch at the Municipal Rose Garden while the spring blooms are at their peak. Let the kids run the paths while you grab some photos. It's free, beautiful, and low-key enough for everyone.
cards.why: April is exactly when this place looks its absolute best.

cards.name: Friday Fun
cards.address: 1157 Minnesota Ave San Jose
cards.blurb: Head over to the San Jose Public Library at 1157 Minnesota Ave for Friday Fun, a free family event built for kids. Show up, let the kids lead, and enjoy a low-pressure afternoon activity that costs nothing.
cards.why: Free, structured fun for kids on a Friday afternoon is hard to beat.

cards.name: Mary's Paleteria Y Neveria & Tasty Traditional Family Flavor
cards.address: 2 N White Rd, San Jose, CA 95127, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Mary's Paleteria for paletas and nieves on the way home. Let the kids pick their flavors and eat outside. It's the perfect end-of-afternoon treat.
cards.why: Nothing wraps up a day with kids better than a cold paleta.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening with a stroll down Santana Row and grab dinner at one of the patio spots along the boulevard. The kids can walk around while you wait for a table. Casual enough for a family Friday night out.
cards.why: It's a nice way to end the day without anyone having to rush home right away.

cards.name: Kenz Coffee Bar @ Oklava
cards.address: 205 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Kenz Coffee Bar, a cozy spot on University Ave. Grab a coffee and settle in before the day kicks off.
cards.why: Great coffee and a relaxed vibe make it a solid way to ease into a Friday.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hit the Dish Trail while the morning is still cool. The climb is real but the views from the top are genuinely worth it — you can see the whole South Bay on a clear day.
cards.why: 72 degrees and clear skies is about as good as it gets for this hike.

cards.name: The Midwife and The Baker
cards.address: 846 Independence Ave, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: The Midwife and the Baker is a Mountain View institution. Get a sandwich on fresh-baked bread and don't leave without picking up something from the pastry case.
cards.why: One of the best lunch spots in the South Bay, and it closes at 2 so you're getting in just in time.

cards.name: Art of Science 2026
cards.address: 475 Via Ortega, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Art of Science 2026 at Stanford turns scientific research into visual art. The images are genuinely stunning and the intersection of disciplines makes for a thought-provoking browse.
cards.why: This kind of exhibition makes you look at both art and science differently.

cards.name: Sweet Diplomacy® Gluten-Free
cards.address: 209 1st St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Sweet Diplomacy in Los Altos is an all-gluten-free bakery that doesn't taste like a compromise at all. Pick up a treat to hold you over before the evening.
cards.why: It's a genuinely special bakery and a nice detour on the way to Los Altos.

cards.name: Books to Blooms: A Garden Lover’s Party
cards.address: 265 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022
cards.blurb: Books to Blooms is a garden lover's party at the Los Altos library. Think books, plants, good company, and a free Friday night vibe you don't have to overthink.
cards.why: Free, festive, and a fun way to wrap up the day in Los Altos.

cards.name: Rootstock Wine Bar
cards.address: 331 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Rootstock Wine Bar in Mountain View. Grab a glass of something interesting from their rotating list and decompress after a full day.
cards.why: Low-key, well-curated, and exactly the kind of place you want on a Friday night.

cards.name: Peninsula Creamery - Dairy Store & Grill
cards.address: 900 High St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Classic diner breakfast at Peninsula Creamery — pancakes, eggs, the works. Kids love it and the prices are totally reasonable.
cards.why: A Palo Alto institution that never disappoints for a laid-back family breakfast.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Explore the Computer History Museum's wild collection of vintage computers, robots, and hands-on exhibits. Kids are usually mesmerized by the giant mechanical calculators and the Babbage Engine.
cards.why: Affordable, genuinely fascinating, and the kind of museum where kids actually want to stay longer.

cards.name: Lagunita BioBlitz
cards.address: Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Join the Lagunita BioBlitz and help document plants, bugs, and wildlife around the lake. Kids get to be actual scientists for a morning, which is pretty great.
cards.why: Hands-on outdoor science for kids on a gorgeous Stanford spring day.

cards.name: Izzy's Brooklyn Bagels
cards.address: 477 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Grab bagel sandwiches at Izzy's Brooklyn Bagels on California Ave. Order a classic lox or egg sandwich and let the kids pick their own.
cards.why: Quick, affordable, and genuinely delicious — a perfect post-adventure lunch.

cards.name: Color Me Mine
cards.address: 321 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Pick out ceramics at Color Me Mine and paint them together as a family. The kids get to take home something they made themselves.
cards.why: It's creative, low-key, and kids absolutely love having a finished piece to bring home.

cards.name: LEGO Fridays!
cards.address: 2300  Wellesley St. Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Head to LEGO Fridays at 2300 Wellesley St and let the kids build whatever they want. Free drop-in fun that'll keep them happily busy.
cards.why: Free LEGO building on a Friday afternoon is basically a guaranteed hit with kids.
